message:

+{

+  "mission": {
+    "title": "helm: Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is keep",
+    "description": "Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is keep. Requested by 64+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current helm deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your helm version and configuration:\n```bash\nhelm list --all-namespaces 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working helm installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review helm configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant helm configuration:\n```bash\nhelm version\n```\nI wanted to know if there is possibility for Helm install command to ignore existing resource (if already created) if the **helm/resource-policy** is **keep**.\n\n**Scenario:**\n1. I created release with Secret containing random generated password."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is\u2026",
+        "description": "Alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7627, https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625, and https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.\n\nHelp with validation would be appreciated, but in theory this closes https://github.com/helm/helm/issues/6850, closes\n```yaml\nKIND=deployment\nNAME=my-app-staging\nRELEASE=staging\nNAMESPACE=default\nkubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE\nkubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE\nkubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nhelm version\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Ignore existing resource on install if resource-policy is\u2026\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Alternative implementation to https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7627, https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7625, and https://github.com/helm/helm/pull/7575.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "KIND=deployment\nNAME=my-app-staging\nRELEASE=staging\nNAMESPACE=default\nkubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-name=$RELEASE\nkubectl annotate $KIND $NAME meta.helm.sh/release-namespace=$NAMESPACE\nkubectl label $KIND $NAME app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm",
+        "apiVersion: apps/v1beta2\nkind: Deployment\nmetadata:\n  name: {{ .Chart.Name}}-deployment-{{ .Values.app.version }}\n  namespace: {{ .Values.namespace }} \nspec:\n  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}\n  selector:\n    matchLabels:\n      app: {{.Chart.Name}}\n  template:\n    metadata:\n      labels:\n        app: {{.Chart.Name}}\n        version: {{ .Values.app.version }}\n    spec:\n      containers:\n        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}\n          image: \"{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Chart.AppVersion }}\"\n          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.image.pullPolicy }}\n          ports:\n            - name: http\n              containerPort: {{ .Values.container.port }}\n---\napiVersion: v1\nkind: Service\nmetadata:\n  name: {{.Chart.Name}}-service\n  namespace: {{ .Values.namespace }}\nspec:\n  ports:\n    - port: {{",
+        "{{- if not (lookup \"rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1\" \"ClusterRole\" \"\" .Values.clusterrole.name) }}\nresources template\n{{- end}}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },

@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "tikv-13673-refine-pd-client",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "tikv: Refine PD client",
+    "description": "Refine PD client. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current tikv deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your tiup --version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nhelm list -n tikv 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working tikv installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review tikv configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant tikv configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nkubectl get configmap -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tikv\n```\n## Development Task\n\nPD client (and PD worker) has been too complicated to be maintained in the past years. There are subtle bugs due to its complicated reconnect mechanism and lock usage."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Refine PD client",
+        "description": "### What is changed and how it works?\n\nIssue Number: Close #13673 \n\nWhat's Changed:\n\n- Move reconnect logic to one single coroutine `reconnect_loop`\n- Move streaming RPCs to individual long-running coroutines that are not destroyed even when connection is rebuilt\n- All client methods take mutable\n```yaml\npub fn hearbeat(&self) -> Sender {\n        let (tx, rx) = tikv_util::futures::bounded();\n        let client = self.cached_client.clone();\n        self.rpc_client.spawn(async move {\n            loop {\n                client.latest().wait_for_ready().await;\n                let (g_tx, g_rx) = client.heartbeat();\n                select!(g_tx.send_all(rx).await, g_rx);\n            }\n        });\n     }\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n tikv -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tikv\nkubectl get events -n tikv --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Refine PD client\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "### What is changed and how it works?\n\nIssue Number: Close #13673 \n\nWhat's Changed:\n\n- Move reconnect logic to one single coroutine `reconnect_loop`\n- Move streaming RPCs to individual long-running coroutines that are not destroyed even when connection is rebuilt\n- All client methods take mutable reference now\n- `PdConnector` and some other constants are shared with v1\n- Old client tests are\n\nSee the source issue and linked PR for the complete solution details.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "pub fn hearbeat(&self) -> Sender {\n        let (tx, rx) = tikv_util::futures::bounded();\n        let client = self.cached_client.clone();\n        self.rpc_client.spawn(async move {\n            loop {\n                client.latest().wait_for_ready().await;\n                let (g_tx, g_rx) = client.heartbeat();\n                select!(g_tx.send_all(rx).await, g_rx);\n            }\n        });\n     }",
+        "### Related changes\n\n### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->\n\nTests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->\n\n- Unit test\n\n\n### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "tikv",
+      "graduated",
+      "storage",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "tikv"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/13673",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/tikv/tikv/pull/13696"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 2,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "tikv-ctl",
+      "tiup"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running TiKV cluster (standalone or deployed on Kubernetes via TiDB Operator). TiUP and tikv-ctl are the primary CLI tools for managing TiKV."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:20:39.218Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
